steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1 establishes that in 1929 scientists assumed progesterone existed only in animals. Sentence 2 reveals that researchers would later find progesterone in corn plants, with the phrase “this discovery wouldn't come until decades later” signaling a forward jump in time. The blank must introduce a later development that disproves the earlier assumption.

steps.title: Test each choice against the time-forward relationship
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'In other words,': signals that Sentence 2 restates Sentence 1 in different terms — but finding progesterone in corn contradicts, not restates, the 1929 assumption. ✗
  • Choice B — 'For example,': signals that Sentence 2 illustrates Sentence 1 with a specific instance — but the corn plant discovery disproves the assumption rather than exemplifying it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Eventually,': signals forward movement to a later point in time — directly matching “wouldn't come until decades later” and introducing the future discovery correctly. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Earlier,': signals a time before Sentence 1 — but the corn plant discovery happened after 1929, not before it. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
